title: New York Fed Releases New Weekly Report on Oil Prices

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York released a new weekly Oil Price Dynamics Report on May 2, 2016, which uses statistical modeling to decompose oil price changes into demand and supply factors. The report was accompanied by a blog post on Liberty Street Economics and was to be released each Monday at 3:00 p.m., except during FOMC blackout periods.
key_points: New York Fed launched Oil Price Dynamics Report on May 2, 2016
Report uses statistical model with financial variables to analyze demand vs. supply factors driving oil prices
Initial highlights noted oil prices rose as demand expectations improved and supply tightened, returning to October 2015 levels
Report noted Q4 2015 oil price weakness was predominantly supply-driven
Report noted Q2-Q3 2015 oil declines were influenced more by global demand expectations than supply
Release schedule: weekly Mondays at 3:00 p.m., excluding FOMC blackout periods
Announcement accompanied by a blog post on Liberty Street Economics
why_it_matters: The record accurately describes the report launch, but the archival value for a market structure archive is questionable given the source's focus on economic research methodology rather than market operations or trading infrastructure.
macro_context: The source discusses how distinguishing between demand-driven and supply-driven oil price changes matters for U.S. economic outlook assessment. The initial report highlights noted improving demand expectations and tightening supply conditions in early 2016.

issues_found: Source is nearly 10 years old (published May 2016) with limited archival value for current market analysis
Topic mismatch: metadata and target specify 'market structure' but source content is about oil price economic research methodology, not market plumbing, trading infrastructure, or operations
Source is an announcement/press release about launching a report, not substantive research content itself
No concrete market structure implications or trading infrastructure details present in source
